prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: A CPU (Central Processing Unit) is like the brain of a computer. Just as our brains help us think, make decisions, and tell our body parts what to do, a CPU helps your computer understand what you want it to do and tells the different parts of the computer how to work together. Imagine you're baking a cake: you (grandchild) give the recipe (instructions) to your grandparent (CPU), and we (grandparent) read and understand it, then we (CPU) tell the electric mixer (hardware) and the oven (other parts of the computer) how to work together to make the cake (run a program). The CPU is the one in charge inside a computer, making sure everything works as it should.
